title: America’s Decade-Long AI War Efforts Get a Trial by Fire
summary: On a recent Friday night, the US made two drastic moves that could end up altering the future of artificial-intelligence-powered warfare. Just after 5 p.m. Eastern time on Feb. 27, Donald Trump’s administration declared that Anthropic PBC, the $380 billion startup whose Claude-branded AI products have recently become ubiquitous, was a supply chain risk. In addition to making consumer-facing chatbots and coding tools, Anthropic had major contracts to provide AI services to the military. That rela

title: Seeing Transformative M&A: Freshfields' Ethan Klingsberg
summary: Freshfields Partner and Co-Head of US Corporate and M&A Ethan Klingsberg discusses navigating regulation, key M&A strategies, and why in the next few months we may see 'transformative M&A.' He talks with Bailey Lipschultz and Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: NASA's Artemis II to Return to Moon in Bet on Agency's Future
summary: Bloomberg's Loren Grush said that NASA's Artemis II mission to get back to the moon is aimed at being a 'proving ground' for potential missions to Mars. She said that NASA hopes to take the lessons learned on the moon and use them to figure out the challenges of living on a surface much farther away. Grush also weighed in on the stakes surrounding NASA's focus on the moon, as the agency tries to prove its worth amid budget concerns and the private space race.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: KPMG Faces Allegations of Blown Audit in Private Credit Collapse
summary: Canada’s top securities regulator alleged that KPMG LLP, the auditor for four funds managed by collapsed private lender Bridging Finance Inc., failed to properly value the loans held within the funds, harming investors.

Gold Posts Worst Month Since 2008 Despite Hopes War Is Near End
summary: Gold logged its worst month since the global financial crisis despite climbing for a third session Tuesday on signs the US and Iran are open to ending the war in the Middle East.
